2605.22007.title: Hallucination as Commitment Failure: Larger LLMs Misfire Despite Knowing the Answer
2605.22007.paper_summary: Hallucination is often viewed as a direct consequence of missing knowledge: a model answers incorrectly when the correct answer is absent from its generation-time distribution, and correctly when it is present. We test this assumption by introducing a semantic notion of answer availability that aggregates token-level variants expressing the same answer concept, and asks whether the correct concept is already available at the moment the model commits to an answer. Across Qwen and Llama models from 0.8B to 72B in both Instruct and Base variants, 16-47% of Instruct hallucinations occur with substantial probability mass already on the correct concept, and the rate rises monotonically with scale. Comparing such failures against correct generations with matched semantic support, the distinguishing factor is not whether the correct concept is represented, but how its probability is distributed: correct generations concentrate mass on a single surface form, hallucinations disperse it across alternatives. The same sharpening asymmetry extends across multi-token generation and is detectable in pre-generation hidden states. Together, these results identify a single mechanism: instruction tuning sharpens answer commitment with scale, making helpfulness and confident hallucination two consequences of the same underlying disposition.
2605.22007.paper_summary_zh: 幻覺常被視為知識缺失的直接後果：當正確答案在其生成時的分佈中缺失時，模型會錯誤回答，而當正確答案存在時則會正確回答。我們通過引入一種語義上可用答案的概念來測試這一假設，該概念聚合表達相同答案概念的標記級變體，並詢問在模型承諾回答的那一刻，正確概念是否已經可用。在Qwen和Llama模型中，從0.8B到72B的Instruct和Base變體中，16-47%的Instruct幻覺在正確概念上已經有相當的概率質量，且隨著規模的增大，這一比率單調上升。將這些失敗與具有匹配語義支持的正確生成進行比較，區分的因素不是正確概念是否被表示，而是其概率的分佈方式：正確生成將質量集中在單一表面形式上，而幻覺則將其分散在替代選項中。同樣的銳化不對稱性延伸到多標記生成中，並且在生成前的隱藏狀態中可檢測到。綜合這些結果，確定了一個單一的機制：指令調整隨著規模的增大而加強答案承諾，使得有用性和自信的幻覺成為同一潛在傾向的兩個後果。
2605.22007.author: Jewon Yeom et.al.
2605.22007.authors: Jewon Yeom, Jaewon Sok, Heejun Kim, Seonghyeon Park, Jeongjae Park, Taesup Kim
